data.description: Total money market funds rose by $50.55b to $7.37t for the week ended October 1st.

data.title: Osara Health named quarterfinalist for Health Equity at 2025 Digital Health Awards, highlighting impact. #HealthTech
data.description: On October 2, 2025, in New York, Osara Health revealed its selection as a Quarterfinalist in the Best in Class track for Health Equity at the 2025 Digital Health Awards. This acknowledgment highlights Osara Health's increasing influence in furthering the cause of health equity.

data.title: Canaan's stock soars post 50,000-rig Bitcoin mining deal. CEO sees renewed US interest in mining.
data.description: The CEO of Canaan has announced that their recent deal for 50,000 rigs is the company's largest in over three years. This significant transaction indicates a growing interest in Bitcoin mining in the United States.

data.title: Amanda Demanda of Amanda Demanda Injury Lawyers featured in Texas Lawyer with article on personal injury law expansion.
data.description: In Houston on October 2, 2025, Amanda Demanda, a lawyer and founder of Amanda Demanda Injury Lawyers, was featured in Texas Lawyer, a leading legal publication in the United States. Her article titled "Beyond the Verdict: A Texas Family, a Tragedy, and the..." was highlighted in the publication. This recognition further solidified Amanda Demanda's reputation in the legal field, reaching a wide audience across the nation.

data.title: National Press Club to screen "The Day Iceland Stood Still" on Oct 8, followed by discussion on gender equality.
data.description: On October 8, 2025, at 6:00 p.m., the National Press Club in Washington will present a screening of "The Day Iceland Stood Still." This will be accompanied by a panel discussion on gender equality featuring renowned filmmakers Pamela Hogan and ...

data.title: In 5-10 years, 1inch co-founder predicts centralized exchanges will act as DeFi front ends as they fade away.
data.description: According to Sergej Kunz, co-founder of 1inch, centralized cryptocurrency exchanges will slowly diminish in importance and primarily act as interfaces for decentralized finance.

data.title: Marksmen Energy Inc. (TSXV: MAH) to resume trading on TSX Venture Exchange after agreeing to hold annual meeting by Dec 31, 2025.
data.description: In Calgary, Alberta on October 2, 2025, Marksmen Energy Inc. (TSXV: MAH) has declared that following the news release on September 2, 2025, its common shares are anticipated to recommence trading on the TSX Venture Exchange (Exchange) in the near future. As part of the Exchange's review process necessary for the Corporation's common shares to restart trading, Marksmen has committed to convene an annual general meeting of its shareholders by December 31, 2025.

data.title: Global Cigar and Cigarillos Market expected to reach USD 120.71 Billion by 2034 with 8.17% CAGR growth rate. Social acceptance and relaxation of regulations are key drivers.
data.description: The global Cigar and Cigarillos Market was valued at approximately USD 55.36 billion in 2024, expected to reach USD 59.88 billion in 2025 and projected to reach around USD 120.71 billion by 2034, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of about 8.17% during the forecast period. Factors driving this growth include the increasing demand for premium tobacco products due to lifestyle trends, rising social acceptance, and relaxed regulations in some economies. In certain regions, regulations have been relaxed, creating promotional opportunities for producers. Growing social acceptance and integration of cigar consumption into celebrations and leisure activities are also key drivers of market growth. The market is expected to witness a significant CAGR between 2025 and 2034. Key segments include product type, flavor, distribution channel, and region. Regional scope covers North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Middle East, Africa, South and Central America. The report offers revenue estimation, company profiles, competitive landscape, growth factors, and recent trends.

data.title: CA Insurance Dept data shows no delay with Consumer Watchdog involvement in rate approvals. Public Citizen opposes regulations.
data.description: In Sacramento, California, as of October 2, 2025, recent information from the California Department of Insurance (CDI) indicates that rate approvals are being processed at a similar or quicker pace when Consumer Watchdog collaborates with the department compared to when the Department operates independently. The data suggests that there is no significant difference in the approval of home insurance rates when Consumer Watchdog is part of the process.

data.title: Roger Kelly named VP of Contracts and Pricing at Newport News Shipbuilding, succeeding Matt Mulherin Jr. in Oct. 2025.
data.description: In Newport News, Virginia on October 2, 2025, HII revealed that Roger Kelly has been elevated to the role of vice president of contracts and pricing at its Newport News Shipbuilding division. This appointment comes as Matt Mulherin Jr. assumes the position of vice president of supply chain and strategic sourcing at NNS. HII is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol HII.

data.title: Teamsters in Maryland authorize strike at Zum Transportation after rejecting contract; members vote overwhelmingly.
data.description: Employees who provide student transportation services for Howard County Public Schools have declined a contract offer. The Teamsters Local 570 members working at Zum Transportation in Jessup, Maryland, have voted against the company's final contract proposal. The workers have authorized a strike as they deemed the terms of the contract to be unacceptable. The decision to reject the offer was made after an overwhelming majority vote by the employees. The specific numbers regarding the proportion of workers in favor of the strike or the details of the contract terms were not disclosed.

data.title: America's Favorite Couple crowned, raising $3,955,341 for LA Regional Food Bank and Oceana. Love, laughter, and generosity prevail.
data.description: In Phoenix on October 2, 2025, a significant event unfolded as the 2025 America's Favorite Couple competition, organized by professional fundraiser Colossal, managed to secure an impressive $3,955,341 in donations. The funds will be utilized to provide support to the Los Angeles Regional Food Bank and Oceana, showcasing a remarkable display of love, laughter, and generosity.

data.title: CenterPoint Energy, Inc. disclosed pricing of tender offers, with a reference yield and total consideration for securities, up to $300 million aggregate purchase price.
data.description: Houston-based company CenterPoint Energy, Inc. (NYSE: CNP) revealed the Reference Yield and Total Consideration for the Securities related to their recent cash tender offers. The Tender Offers target a total aggregate purchase price of up to $300 million (excluding Accrued Interest).

data.title: Toll Brothers introduces luxury home community in Santa Rosa Valley, California with equestrian sites.
data.description: A prestigious gated community is now offering luxurious homes situated on spacious equestrian home sites. Residents can enjoy exclusive access to upscale amenities and services within the community. With a limited number of available homes, the development is designed for those seeking a high-end lifestyle in a peaceful setting. The properties feature premium finishes and top-of-the-line fixtures, catering to the discerning tastes of potential buyers. The community boasts a serene environment, perfect for horse enthusiasts and those looking for a sophisticated living experience. Contact us now for more details on this unique opportunity to reside in a truly elite community.

data.title: Intel stock has surged 50% in the last month, reaching a total value of roughly $16 billion for the government's stake.
data.description: Intel Corp's stock (NASDAQ: INTC) has maintained its strong momentum, with a 3.9% increase on Thursday, leading to a remarkable monthly gain of over 50%. The current value of the U.S. government's 10% equity stake in Intel has surged significantly to around $16 billion. This surge in value has been influenced by the company's rising stock prices. The federal government initially acquired a stake in Intel in August. For further details, please refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Squamish municipal workers from CUPE Local 2269 starting job action for fair contract due to wage issues after year of negotiations.
data.description: In Squamish, employees from CUPE Local 2269, representing District of Squamish workers, are preparing for rotating job action starting this morning. Following almost a year of negotiations, workers have not achieved a satisfactory agreement, particularly on the topic of competitive wages that align with the increasing cost of living. "Initiating job action is a final measure," stated Celeste Bickford, president of CUPE Local 2269. The members are reluctant to strike, but they are insisting on fair wages.
